text: There have been longstanding issues with metal contamination when using traditional ATRP. We have developed a metal-free catalytic technique to achieve ATRP under photonic activation using an organic photoredox catalyst as the transfer agent. Under activation and deactivation by light, the vinyl monomers are efficiently polymerised and growth and termination of the polymer chains are strictly controlled, resulting in narrow polydispersities, well-defined molecular weights and precise chain ends. In addition, the synthesis of block copolymers is achievable, and can be further combined with other methods of controlled radical polymerisation to produce a range of advanced polymer architectures. We believe that the use of our organic photoredox catalysts in controlled radical polymerisation, as well as their application to small molecule transformations and polymer chemistry, will significantly open the scope of new materials that can be produced.
